title: Optionally passing credentials to mirrors
body:
The following code introduced by https://github.com/containers/image/pull/625 will reset the auth credentials for a registry if a mirror is tried, because 
`reference.Domain(physicalRef.ref) != reference.Domain(logicalRef.ref)`:

https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/71ca11ca55f00cbf9c033f29f6d0cbafb76415e4/image/docker/docker_image_src.go#L139-L145


CRI-O is experimenting with [Kubelet Credential providers](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/kubelet-credential-provider/) to fulfill the following use case:

1. Admin creates the mirror configuration using IDMS/ITMS/ICSP (`registries.conf`) to map `registry.local` to `mirror.local`
1. Admin creates an image pull secret for `mirror.local` 
1. User creates pod to pull image from `registry.local`
1. Credential provider resolves auth data for `mirror.local` via `registries.conf` and provides that to the kubelet
1. CRI-O will use the credentials for `mirror.local` for the pull

CRI-O will now try the mirror first, but will not pass down the credentials because of the above code part. 

Would it be possible to make this behavior optional?

I see a risk of potentially leaking credentials from one registry to another, but for air gapped environments users can be made aware of that. A PoC for that mentioned credential provider is available in https://github.com/saschagrunert/credential-provider.

cc @QiWang19 @haircommander

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

I don’t think it makes any sense to _double down_ on the behavior where credentials are just user:password with no registry specification, and “deal” with that by a warning in a documentation. (Like we do in the unqualified search vs. Kubelet situation.) 

It’s not just that a credential for one mirror might be sent to another mirror, a credential for a mirror might be sent to the primary registry, or a credential for a primary registry to the mirror.

That endangers users, and is just not a good feature. At the very least, we should _try_ for a design that works as naturally expected.

---

Presumably the original data, in traditional k8s pull secrets, does contain the host name; so this should be a matter of getting the (host, credentials) pairs from A to B.

Right now, this works by setting `types.SystemContext.AuthFilePath` to a _local file_ that contains all the (host, credentials) data. E.g. for `podman-remote`, each operation creates such a temporary file, and passes that to c/image.

I think that should be a viable approach, working against the current c/image API, and the first thing I’d recommend trying.

That said, there are, by now, several Go callers that have the data in a Go structure, and are forced to write such a temporary file. So, I think it does make sense to add a new API to c/image, a replacement for `DockerAuthConfig`+`DockerBearerRegistryToken` that allows specifying individual credentials for mirrors, and hopefully is extensible to other kinds of credentials.

Thank you for the clarification! I think writing an auth file from the credential provider into a directory per namespace would work. We have to consider possible races, but the overall approach seems viable to me. I guess we can then also avoid having a new API from that perspective.
I was thinking a single-use temporary file, but whatever fits your use case.

> I was thinking a single-use temporary file, but whatever fits your use case.

Yes, per Kubernetes namespace. Do you know by the top of your head if we have to merge the global auth file with `types.SystemContext.AuthFilePath` in CRI-O?
Yes; if `AuthFilePath` is set, no other paths are read. Merging that does sound like a hassle…
